name: Federal Reserve
summary: Day 72 Sunday (markets closed; Friday May 8 close still operative). **DXY 97.84 (-0.4% 1D, -1.0% 1M); RIEDER FED-CHAIR SPECULATION + TRUMP COMFORTABLE-WITH-WEAK-DOLLAR COMMENTS = DOLLAR-WEAKNESS REGIME INTACT.** US 10Y 4.36% (-1.4% 1D, +0.3% 1M). VIX 17.39 cycle-low. Today's metals rally (gold +1.1%, silver +2.9%, copper +1.0%) is dollar-driven not war-risk — cross-asset confirmation: VIX low, S&P record, defense weak, yields lower. **BlackRock's Rick Rieder (vocal advocate of aggressive rate cuts) emerging as leading candidate for next Fed chair → dovish-Fed expectation builds.** If Rieder confirmed/announced as next Fed chair, dollar-weakness regime confirmed → multi-quarter EM-bullish setup including Indian equities. Brent collapse to ~$103 May 6 + World Bank $86 average for 2026 + Goldman Q2 $90 reduces inflation pass-through pressure on Fed; Powell's April 'it hasn't even peaked yet' framing now structurally contradicted by post-MOU energy-price-decline trajectory. CME June cut probability ~28%; Polymarket ~56% zero 2026 cuts — but the rate path could now reprice dovish if MOU signs and Brent settles at $86-90. **The Fed's biggest single dovish catalyst is now MOU signature** — institutional energy-inflation acceptance becomes inverse of the kinetic-restart pricing.

recent_signals.headline: CME FEDWATCH JUNE CUT 28% / HOLD 70%; POLYMARKET 56.4% PROBABILITY ZERO 2026 CUTS
FOMC HELD 3.5-3.75% WITH FOUR-WAY DISSENT (MOST SPLINTERED SINCE OCT 1992); POWELL: 'IT HASN'T EVEN PEAKED YET' ON ENERG

historical_summaries.summary: 4 signals. FOMC voted 11-1 to hold rates at 3 | Market removed basically every rate cut for 2026, now pricing odds of a HIKE | Fed remains trapped -- 10Y at 4
19 signals. Eurozone inflation surged to 2 | Fed trapped in stagflation -- ISM Prices Index at 78 | DXY fell to 99
